summary: StrongDM canonical page describing DTU as behavioral clones of Okta/Jira/Slack/Google services for high-volume scenario validation without API costs or rate limits.
Index of StrongDM's named techniques for non-interactive agentic development; introduces the Validation Constraint that systems must be validatable without semantic source inspection.

Anthropic Red Team writeup of an autonomous Claude agent that infers properties from docstrings/types and writes Hypothesis tests, finding bugs in NumPy, SciPy, Pandas; published at NeurIPS 2025 DL4C workshop.

Birgitta Bockeler defines harness engineering as guides+sensors regulating agent behavior, distinguishing computational (deterministic linters/tests) from inferential (LLM judge) checks across maintainability, architecture-fitness, and behaviour harnesses.
Stan Hamara introduces cagent's VCR-pattern session recording: record once against real LLM/tool providers, replay deterministically from YAML cassettes for CI testing with zero API cost.
ArXiv paper (Maaz, DeVoe, Hatfield-Dodds, Carlini) — LLM agent autonomously synthesizes Hypothesis property tests; 56% bug-report validity, 86% on top-21 bugs, 3 patches merged including NumPy.

Empirical SWE-bench Verified study finds agent self-written tests function as debug print statements rather than validation; prompt-induced changes in test volume produce no significant change in resolution rate, undermining 'agent-tests-itself' justification.

Record-and-replay framework for LLM agents that captures interaction traces and decision processes, summarizes them into multi-level experience abstractions, and replays with check functions as safety validation.

Anthropic engineering post on agent eval design covering deterministic graders, model-based rubrics, balanced problem sets, and pass@k / pass^k metrics that explicitly accommodate non-deterministic agent behavior.

Aaron Eline (Kiro) shows spec-driven development translating natural-language requirements into Hypothesis property tests with shrinking-based counterexamples, framed as a neurosymbolic loop validating LLM-generated code.

Stanford CodeX critique of StrongDM's approach: agents-test-agents share blind spots (Russell alignment problem); 'satisfaction' metric still uses LLM-as-judge of the same model class as the coder, perpetuating circularity.

Critical assessment citing CodeRabbit Dec 2025 study (1.4-1.7x more critical issues in AI code) and Replit/Moltbook incidents; argues the holdout-set defense fails for unanticipated catastrophic failures.

Andrew Stevens enumerates seven primitives for deterministic replay (structured trace, stable metadata, replay engine, deterministic stubs, harness DI, governance, regression) framed as audit/forensics infrastructure for autonomous agents.

Practitioner field report applying StrongDM-style validation to a personal site (works) and a Rails monolith (only Level 3 achievable); proposes 'micro-factories' with bounded surface area as a transitional pattern.

Long-form interview with OpenAI Frontier's Ryan Lopopolo on shipping a 1M-LOC system with 0% human-written / 0% human-reviewed code via deterministic CI gates and dependency-direction enforcement; companion to Feb 11 2026 OpenAI post.

Vendor explainer summarizing the deterministic-vs-probabilistic constraint distinction and a three-layer pattern (constraint harness, feedback loops, quality gates); references Apiiro's Sep 2025 finding that AI-generated code added 10k+ security issues per month.
StrongDM's official narrative of their software factory: scenario-based validation, DTU, autonomous end-to-end agent execution, with validation cycles replacing human code review.
Top-level page positioning the factory as 'non-interactive development where specs + scenarios drive agents that write code, run harnesses, and converge without human review.'

Synthesis emphasizing rigorous test infra over human oversight: holdout-stored scenarios, digital twins, hard guardrails (linters/formatters as preconditions), QA agents running thousands of scenarios separated from coding agents.
Open-source eval harness documentation: trajectory grading, deterministic test execution, model-graded rubrics, and assertion patterns specifically for coding agents.

ICLR 2026 paper introducing HAL: standardized harness running 21,730 rollouts across 9 models and 9 benchmarks; reliability dashboard scores consistency, predictability, robustness, safety, self-awareness beyond accuracy.

ICSE 2026 paper defining a domain-specific language of (trigger, predicate, enforcement) rules to constrain agent actions at runtime; prevents >90% of unsafe code-agent executions with millisecond overhead.
Reference implementation of AgentSpec runtime enforcement for LLM agents; companion to the ICSE 2026 paper.
PyPI package providing record/replay for MCP JSON-RPC interactions, enabling deterministic agent tests without a live MCP server.
Trade-press summary of cagent's deterministic-replay value proposition; useful as third-party confirmation that the cassette pattern is being adopted as a CI primitive for agent tests.
